Which one is not an adverb?
Which of the following sentences is correct?
options_html: Not only they helped the victims, they donated money too.
Not only did they helped the victims, they donated money too.
Not only did they help the victims, they donated money too.
Not only they did help the victims, they donated money too.
correct_answer_html: Not only did they help the victims, they donated money too.

question_html: ‘All hats and no cows’ refers to-
The passive form of ‘She plucked some flowers’ is-
options_html: Some flowers are plucked by her.
Some flowers were plucked by her.
Some flowers had been plucked by her.
correct_answer_html: Some flowers were plucked by her.

question_html: “Thank you so much.” Choose the correct indirect speech.
options_html: He told thank you so much.
He told that he thanked me so much.
He told me that I should be thanked.

question_html: “As soon as I wanted to catch the bird, it flew away.” Transform the sentence into negative sentence.
options_html: No sooner had I wanted to catch the bird then it flew away.
No sooner had I wanted to catch the bird than it flew away.
No sooner had I wanted to catch the bird than it flown away.
No sooner I had wanted to catch the bird that it flew away.
correct_answer_html: No sooner had I wanted to catch the bird than it flew away.

question_html: At no time ____ the rules.
Make it negative: “He is always punctual.”
Change the narration: He said, “Would that I were a child again.”
options_html: He wished that he had been a child again.
He wish that he had been a child again.
He wished that he would be a child again.
He said that he were a child again.
correct_answer_html: He wished that he had been a child again.

question_html: “I met her a long time ago but she gave me the cold shoulder.” – The underlined phrase means is-
Among these sentences, which one contains ‘the’ as an adverb?
options_html: The boy who came here is my cousin.
Every day I water the plants.

question_html: Change the voice: “Do it.”
Change the narration: He told me, "What is your name?"
options_html: He asked what my name is.
He asked what was my name.
He asked what my name was.
correct_answer_html: He asked what my name was.

question_html: Make it interrogative: “He is nothing but a liar.”
options_html: Is he anything but a liar?
What else is he but a liar?
He is nothing but a liar?
correct_answer_html: Is he anything but a liar?

question_html: She said, “What do you want?” (Change the narration)
options_html: She asked what did I want.
She asked what do I want.
